nodes.title: SL:Fmly Schl & Cmty Collab
nodes.description: Provides a foundation for understanding basic concepts regarding home, school, and community collaboration. Focuses specifically on creating partnerships between diverse families, families whose children have disabilities, and community partners and schools that serve these populations. Prerequisites: ECLD 1560, EDSP 1050; or Instructor permission. Pre/Co-requisite: ECLD 2020.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Pre/Co-requisite: ECLD 2020. Prereqs enforced by the system: ECLD 1560 and EDSP 1050; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Iss Aff Persons W/Disabil
nodes.description: Students study the effects of discrimination, advocacy, litigation and sociological perspectives on disabilities. History, current legislation, and family issues for children and adults are emphasized.
nodes.other_requirements: Only open to CESS first-time first-years or by instructor permission; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: ELL Practicum
nodes.description: A practicum opportunity for Education majors who intend to pursue the Multilingual Learner (ML) Endorsement for grades PreK-6, 7-12, or PreK-12. Assignments include weekly reflections, informal lessons, and resource building for teaching/tutoring multilingual learners. Prerequisite: ECLD 1560.
nodes.other_requirements: Contact instructor for location and time. Lab fee: $40.00.

nodes.title: Lang Policy Issues,Race&Sch
nodes.description: Explores the theories, practices, and policies related to race and language, and how they impact the working, teaching, and learning of English learners in multilingual and multicultural settings.

nodes.title: Bilingual Education & Policy
nodes.description: Examines the foundation of educational policy as it relates to bilingual education and program planning in grades K-12 in U.S. schools. Review English language theory, as well as state and federal policy. Prerequisite: ECLD 1560.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Developing Curriculum for ELs
nodes.description: Prepares students who intend to teach in a K-12 classroom environment or similar setting by exploring language acquisition theories, instructional methods, and lesson planning for multilingual learners of English. Prerequisites: ECLD 1560; ECLD 2020; minimum Junior standing; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing; or Instructor permission

nodes.title: SL:Rlating/Rspnding ToCmntyNds
nodes.description: Students engage directly with community organizations or schools to provide services identified through conversations with community partners. In addition to field work, students engage in modules and course meetings to guide their learning, critical reflection, and the creation of a semester-long project in service to their host. Prerequisites: ECLD 1560 or EDSP 1050. Cross-listed with: EDSP 3040.
nodes.other_requirements: ECLD (ECL1, ECL2) minors only or by instructor permission.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
